title: Music Department Annual Update - 2022-2023 SY (Part 1)
text:
College of the Siskiyous Music Department Annual Update 2022-2023
Prepared by – Ron Slabbinck, Ed.D., Music Faculty
Reviewed/Edited by – Valerie Roberts, Dean, Liberal Arts
1. Describe annual progress / changes / events in the program.
A. Most significant challenges
– Continued Implications of COVID-19
– Reduction in class offerings (ensembles) and reduction in student enrollment
– Uncertainty surrounding student/community course interest
– Safety returning in person, protocols, masks, music making as a risk of disease
spread
B. Most significant growth/learning
– Expanding on growth from last year regarding COVID-19
– Continue to develop non-traditional means for music instruction (traditionally face-
to-face adapted to remote)
– More courses now able to be taught remotely
– New pedagogy and instruction techniques discovered in remote instruction can be
applied to traditional face-to-face instruction
– Creatively integrate online and in person (hybrid)
– Leverage Technology for music instruction
2. Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program.
- SLOs are informally assessed. The college has an official means to collect and report
this information, and planning for faculty training and piloting began. This was put
on hold and details are being negotiated.
3. Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program.
Obstacles (mostly the same from last Annual Update)
- Hesitancy to fully return face to face following COVID-19
- Shrinking Local K-12 music programs
o COVID
o budget constraints
o diminishing student population
o programs closing
o recent and upcoming K-12 Music Faculty retirements
o uncertainty surrounding support for music in K-12 districts
- Inconsistent College Budget and continued uncertainty surrounding college budget

-- 1 of 2 --

title: Music Department Annual Update - 2022-2023 SY (Part 2)
text:
o How many classes being offered? And which classes?
o Will travel be reinstated?
o Will ensembles be at/near full capacity?
o What is behind in being replaced/repaired/maintained?
Support
- Dean and Vice President of Academic Affairs
o Understand value of music and the arts
o Supportive of creative scheduling/course offerings
- Adjunct Faculty member added last year
o Continues to add more flexibility in course offerings and times
o Functions as both Adjunct Instructor and EIA for ensembles and music majors
o Has enabled the department to bring back ensembles
▪ Concert Band
▪ Chamber Music Ensemble
4. Describe program budgetary needs or implications. (Submit a CQIP if you are requesting a
budget allocation that falls outside of regular yearly budget allocations).
All budget items/areas not reinstated or increased from previous Annual updates and Program
Reviews. CQIPs will be submitted to resolve all of the line items that had been zeroed out or
reduced. These could include (budget only recently accurate and available):
• 2126 Class-Overtime
• 2211 Instr Aide (Instr, Reg) and 2411 Instructional Aide - Direct
• 2321 Student Help (Noninstr) - District
• 4130 Instructional Supplies - Other
• 4140 A.V. Supplies (Instr)
• 4180 Software (Instr)
• 4250 Maint/Repair Supplies (Noninstr)
• 5110 Guest Lecturer and 5190 Consultant - Other
• 5211 Trvl - Acad (Instr, Reg) and 5213 Trvl - Acad (Noninstr, Reg)
• 5223 Trvl - Class (Noninstr, Reg)
• 5550 Laundry Services
• 5612 Rent - Bldg/Facility
• 5633 Maintenance & Repair – Equipment
• 5910 Printing/Advertising (Nonreq'd)
• 5932 Activity Costs (Instr)
• 5939 Misc Other - Instr Services
• 5963 Environmental Safety
• 5975 Mileage - Field Trips
• 88713 Community Service Fee
